title.raw: 《黥布列傳》 Biography of Qing Bu

sentences.zh: 黥布者，六人也，姓英氏。

translations.literal: Qing Bu was a man of Liu, surnamed Ying.
translations.idiomatic: Qing Bu was a native of Liu, with the surname Ying.

sentences.zh: 秦時為布衣。

translations.literal: During the Qin dynasty he was a commoner.
translations.idiomatic: During the Qin era, he lived as a commoner.

sentences.zh: 少年，有客相之曰：「當刑而王。」

translations.literal: When young, a traveling physiognomist read his features and said: 'You shall be punished and then become a king.'
translations.idiomatic: In his youth, a traveling physiognomist examined his features and declared, "You shall first suffer punishment, and then become a king."

sentences.zh: 及壯，坐法黥。

translations.literal: When he reached manhood, he was convicted of a crime and tattooed on the face.
translations.idiomatic: When he reached manhood, he was convicted of a crime and branded with the convict's tattoo.

sentences.zh: 布欣然笑曰；

translations.literal: Bu happily laughed and said,
translations.idiomatic: Bu laughed with delight and said,

sentences.zh: 「人相我當刑而王，幾是乎？」

translations.literal: 'Someone read my physiognomy and said I would be punished and then become king—could this be the beginning?'
translations.idiomatic: "Someone once read my face and foretold that I would suffer punishment before becoming a king. Could this be the start of that prophecy?"

sentences.zh: 人有聞者，共俳笑之。

translations.literal: Those who heard this all mocked and laughed at him.
translations.idiomatic: Those who overheard him mocked and laughed at his words.

sentences.zh: 布已論輸麗山，麗山之徒數十萬人，布皆與其徒長豪桀交通，乃率其曹偶，亡之江中為群盜。

translations.literal: Bu had already been sentenced to labor at Mount Li. Among the hundreds of thousands of convicts at Mount Li, Bu associated with all the leaders and outstanding men among the convicts, and then led his group of companions to flee to the Yangtze River area to become bandits.
translations.idiomatic: After Bu was sentenced to hard labor at Mount Li, he found himself among hundreds of thousands of convicts. There he befriended every leader and formidable figure among the laborers, and eventually led a band of his companions to escape to the Yangtze River region, where they turned to banditry.

sentences.zh: 陳勝之起也，布乃見番君，與其眾叛秦，聚兵數千人。

translations.literal: When Chen Sheng rose in revolt, Bu went to see Lord Fan, and together with his followers rebelled against Qin, gathering several thousand soldiers.
translations.idiomatic: When Chen Sheng raised the banner of revolt, Bu sought out Lord Fan of Poyang and joined forces with his followers to rebel against Qin, assembling an army of several thousand men.

sentences.zh: 番君以其女妻之。

translations.literal: Lord Fan gave his daughter to him in marriage.
translations.idiomatic: Lord Fan gave him his daughter in marriage.

sentences.zh: 章邯之滅陳勝，破呂臣軍，布乃引兵北擊秦左右校，破之清波，引兵而東。

translations.literal: When Zhang Han destroyed Chen Sheng and defeated Lü Chen's army, Bu led his troops north to attack the Qin left and right battalions, defeated them at Qingbo, and marched his army eastward.
translations.idiomatic: After Zhang Han destroyed Chen Sheng and routed the army of Lü Chen, Bu led his forces northward to attack the Qin left and right battalions. He defeated them at Qingbo, then marched his army eastward.

sentences.zh: 聞項梁定江東會稽，涉江而西。

translations.literal: Hearing that Xiang Liang had pacified Jiangdong and Kuaiji, he crossed the Yangtze River and went west.
translations.idiomatic: Upon hearing that Xiang Liang had established control over Jiangdong and Kuaiji, he crossed the Yangtze and headed west to join him.

sentences.zh: 陳嬰以項氏世為楚將，乃以兵屬項梁，渡淮南，英布、蒲將軍亦以兵屬項梁。

translations.literal: Chen Ying, because the Xiang clan had served as Chu generals for generations, submitted his troops to Xiang Liang and crossed south of the Huai River. Ying Bu and General Pu also submitted their troops to Xiang Liang.
translations.idiomatic: Chen Ying, recognizing that the Xiang family had served as generals of Chu for generations, placed his forces under Xiang Liang's command and crossed south of the Huai River. Ying Bu and General Pu likewise pledged their troops to Xiang Liang.

sentences.zh: 項梁涉淮而西，擊景駒、秦嘉等，布常冠軍。

translations.literal: Xiang Liang crossed the Huai River and went west, attacking Jing Ju, Qin Jia, and others. Bu was always foremost in the army.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang crossed the Huai River and pushed westward, attacking Jing Ju, Qin Jia, and their allies. In every engagement, Bu distinguished himself as the foremost warrior.

sentences.zh: 項梁至薛，聞陳王定死，迺立楚懷王。

translations.literal: When Xiang Liang reached Xue, he heard that King Chen had certainly died, and so established King Huai of Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Liang arrived at Xue, he received confirmation of King Chen's death and thereupon enthroned King Huai of Chu.

sentences.zh: 項梁號為武信君，英布為當陽君。

translations.literal: Xiang Liang was titled Lord Wuxin, and Ying Bu was titled Lord Dangyang.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Liang took the title of Lord Wuxin, and Ying Bu was granted the title of Lord Dangyang.

sentences.zh: 項梁敗死定陶，懷王徙都彭城，諸將英布亦皆保聚彭城。

translations.literal: Xiang Liang was defeated and killed at Dingtao. King Huai moved the capital to Pengcheng, and the various generals including Ying Bu all gathered and held at Pengcheng.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Liang was defeated and killed at Dingtao, King Huai relocated the capital to Pengcheng. The generals, Ying Bu among them, all withdrew and regrouped at Pengcheng.

sentences.zh: 當是時，秦急圍趙，趙數使人請救。

translations.literal: At that time, Qin urgently besieged Zhao, and Zhao repeatedly sent envoys to request rescue.
translations.idiomatic: At that time, the Qin army had tightened its siege around Zhao, and Zhao sent envoy after envoy pleading for relief.

sentences.zh: 懷王使宋義為上將，范曾為末將，項籍為次將，英在、蒲將軍皆為將軍，悉屬宋義，北救趙。

translations.literal: King Huai appointed Song Yi as supreme general, Fan Zeng as the rear general, and Xiang Ji as the deputy general. Ying Bu and General Pu were both made generals, all placed under Song Yi's command, and sent north to rescue Zhao.
translations.idiomatic: King Huai appointed Song Yi as supreme commander, Fan Zeng as rear general, and Xiang Ji as deputy commander. Ying Bu and General Pu were both given generalships, and all were placed under Song Yi's authority with orders to march north and relieve Zhao.

sentences.zh: 及項籍殺宋義於河上，懷王因立籍為上將軍，諸將皆屬項籍。

translations.literal: When Xiang Ji killed Song Yi at the river, King Huai then appointed Ji as supreme general, and all the generals were placed under Xiang Ji's command.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Ji killed Song Yi by the river, King Huai had no choice but to appoint Ji as supreme commander. All the generals were placed under his authority.

sentences.zh: 項籍使布先渡河擊秦，布數有利，籍迺悉引兵涉河從之，遂破秦軍，降章邯等。

translations.literal: Xiang Ji sent Bu to cross the river first and attack Qin. Bu won several engagements, and Ji then led all his forces across the river to follow him. They thus destroyed the Qin army and accepted the surrender of Zhang Han and others.
translations.idiomatic: Xiang Ji sent Bu across the river first to engage the Qin forces. Bu scored several victories, after which Ji led the full army across the river to join him. Together they crushed the Qin army and accepted the surrender of Zhang Han and his officers.

sentences.zh: 楚兵常勝，功冠諸侯。

translations.literal: The Chu army was always victorious, and its achievements surpassed all the other lords.
translations.idiomatic: The Chu forces were victorious in every campaign, their achievements surpassing those of all the other feudal lords.

sentences.zh: 諸侯兵皆以服屬楚者，以布數以少敗眾也。

translations.literal: The reason the armies of all the lords submitted to and served Chu was that Bu had repeatedly defeated larger forces with smaller ones.
translations.idiomatic: The reason every feudal army submitted to Chu was that Bu had time and again defeated vastly superior forces with inferior numbers.

sentences.zh: 項籍之引兵西至新安，又使布等夜擊阬章邯秦卒二十餘萬人。

translations.literal: When Xiang Ji led his army west to Xin'an, he again sent Bu and others to attack at night and bury alive more than two hundred thousand Qin soldiers under Zhang Han.
translations.idiomatic: When Xiang Ji led his army westward to Xin'an, he ordered Bu and others to carry out a nighttime massacre, burying alive over two hundred thousand of Zhang Han's surrendered Qin soldiers.

sentences.zh: 至關，不得入，又使布等先從閒道破關下軍，遂得入，至咸陽。

translations.literal: When they reached the pass and could not enter, he again sent Bu and others to go first through a side road to defeat the army below the pass, and thus gained entry, reaching Xianyang.
translations.idiomatic: When they arrived at the pass and found it blocked, Xiang Ji sent Bu and others ahead through a hidden route to smash the defending garrison below the pass. With the way cleared, the army entered and advanced to Xianyang.

sentences.zh: 布常為軍鋒。

translations.literal: Bu always served as the vanguard of the army.
translations.idiomatic: In every campaign, Bu served as the army's vanguard.

sentences.zh: 項王封諸將，立布為九江王，都六。

translations.literal: The King of Xiang enfeoffed the various generals and established Bu as King of Jiujiang, with his capital at Liu.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Xiang enfeoffed the generals, he made Bu the King of Jiujiang and established his capital at Liu.

sentences.zh: 漢元年四月，諸侯皆罷戲下，各就國。

translations.literal: In the fourth month of the first year of Han, the lords all disbanded from below Xi and each went to his own kingdom.
translations.idiomatic: In the fourth month of the first year of the Han dynasty, the feudal lords all disbanded from the assembly at Xi and departed for their respective kingdoms.

sentences.zh: 項氏立懷王為義帝，徙都長沙，乃陰令九江王布等行擊之。

translations.literal: The Xiang clan established King Huai as the Righteous Emperor and relocated his capital to Changsha, then secretly ordered the King of Jiujiang Bu and others to go and attack him.
translations.idiomatic: The Xiang clan elevated King Huai to the title of Righteous Emperor and relocated his capital to Changsha. Then they secretly ordered the King of Jiujiang, Bu, and others to go and attack him.

sentences.zh: 其八月，布使將擊義帝，追殺之郴縣。

translations.literal: In the eighth month, Bu sent a general to attack the Righteous Emperor, chasing and killing him at Chen County.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth month, Bu dispatched a general to attack the Righteous Emperor, who was pursued and killed at Chen County.

sentences.zh: 漢二年，齊王田榮畔楚，項王往擊齊，徵兵九江，九江王布稱病不往，遣將將數千人行。

translations.literal: In the second year of Han, the King of Qi, Tian Rong, rebelled against Chu. The King of Xiang went to attack Qi and requisitioned troops from Jiujiang, but the King of Jiujiang, Bu, claimed illness and did not go, sending a general with only a few thousand men.
translations.idiomatic: In the second year of Han, Tian Rong, the King of Qi, rebelled against Chu. The King of Xiang marched to attack Qi and demanded troops from Jiujiang, but Bu, the King of Jiujiang, pleaded illness and refused to go in person, dispatching only a general with several thousand men.

sentences.zh: 漢之敗楚彭城，布又稱病不佐楚。

translations.literal: When the Han defeated Chu at Pengcheng, Bu again claimed illness and did not assist Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When Han attacked and defeated Chu at Pengcheng, Bu once again pleaded illness and refused to come to Chu's aid.

sentences.zh: 項王由此怨布，數使使者誚讓召布，布愈恐，不敢往。

translations.literal: The King of Xiang from this point resented Bu, and repeatedly sent envoys to rebuke and summon Bu. Bu grew ever more fearful and did not dare to go.
translations.idiomatic: From this point on, the King of Xiang bore a grudge against Bu. He repeatedly dispatched envoys to reprimand and summon him, but Bu only grew more fearful and dared not comply.

sentences.zh: 項王方北憂齊、趙，西患漢，所與者獨九江王，又多布材，欲親用之，以故未擊。

translations.literal: The King of Xiang was worried about Qi and Zhao in the north and troubled by Han in the west. His only remaining ally was the King of Jiujiang, and he moreover valued Bu's abilities and wished to use him personally, so he had not yet attacked him.
translations.idiomatic: The King of Xiang was preoccupied with threats from Qi and Zhao in the north and from Han in the west. His sole remaining ally was the King of Jiujiang, and he prized Bu's military talent and wished to retain his services. For these reasons, he held off from attacking him.

sentences.zh: 漢三年，漢王擊楚，大戰彭城，不利，出梁地，至虞，謂左右曰：「如彼等者，無足與計天下事。」

translations.literal: In the third year of Han, the King of Han attacked Chu and fought a great battle at Pengcheng, but the result was unfavorable. He withdrew through the Liang territory and reached Yu, where he said to his attendants: 'Men such as those are not worth consulting about the affairs of the empire.'
translations.idiomatic: In the third year of Han, the King of Han attacked Chu and fought a great battle at Pengcheng, but suffered defeat. He retreated through the territory of Liang until he reached Yu, where he said to his attendants, "Men like these are not worth consulting about the affairs of the empire."

sentences.zh: 謁者隨何進曰：「不審陛下所謂。」

translations.literal: The court attendant Sui He stepped forward and said: 'I do not understand what Your Majesty means.'
translations.idiomatic: The palace attendant Sui He stepped forward and said, "I do not understand what Your Majesty means by this."

sentences.zh: 漢王曰：「孰能為我使淮南，令之發兵倍楚，留項王於齊數月，我之取天下可以百全。」

translations.literal: The King of Han said: 'Who can serve as my envoy to Huainan, persuade him to raise troops and betray Chu, and pin the King of Xiang down in Qi for several months? Then my conquest of the empire would be all but certain.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Han replied, "Who can go as my envoy to the King of Huainan and persuade him to raise his army and turn against Chu, thereby pinning the King of Xiang in Qi for several months? If that could be done, my conquest of the empire would be assured."

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「臣請使之。」

translations.literal: Sui He said: 'I request to be sent as envoy.'
translations.idiomatic: Sui He said, "Allow me to go as your envoy."

sentences.zh: 乃與二十人俱，使淮南。

translations.literal: He then went together with twenty men on a mission to Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: He set out with a party of twenty men on the mission to Huainan.

sentences.zh: 至，因太宰主之，三日不得見。

translations.literal: When they arrived, the Grand Steward received them, but for three days they were not granted an audience.
translations.idiomatic: Upon their arrival, the Grand Steward received them, but for three days they were denied an audience with the king.

sentences.zh: 隨何因說太宰曰：「王之不見何，必以楚為彊，以漢為弱，此臣之所以為使。

translations.literal: Sui He then persuaded the Grand Steward, saying: 'The reason the king will not see me is surely because he considers Chu strong and Han weak. This is precisely why I have come as envoy.
translations.idiomatic: Sui He then addressed the Grand Steward, saying, "The reason the king refuses to see me is surely that he considers Chu strong and Han weak. But this is precisely why I have been sent here.

sentences.zh: 使何得見，言之而是邪，是大王所欲聞也；

translations.literal: If you allow me an audience and what I say is right, then this is what the Great King wishes to hear;
translations.idiomatic: If I am granted an audience and my arguments prove sound, then the king will hear what he needs to hear.

sentences.zh: 言之而非邪，使何等二十人伏斧質淮南市，以明王倍漢而與楚也。」

translations.literal: If what I say is wrong, then let me and my twenty men submit to the executioner's axe in the Huainan marketplace, to demonstrate that the king rejects Han and sides with Chu.'
translations.idiomatic: If my arguments prove wrong, then let my twenty companions and me lay our necks upon the executioner's block in the Huainan marketplace, as a public declaration that the king has rejected Han and chosen Chu."

sentences.zh: 太宰乃言之王，王見之。

translations.literal: The Grand Steward then reported this to the king, and the king received him.
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Steward relayed this to the king, who then agreed to receive him.

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「漢王使臣敬進書大王御者，竊怪大王與楚何親也。」

translations.literal: Sui He said: 'The King of Han has sent me to respectfully present a letter to Your Majesty. I privately wonder what makes Your Majesty so close to Chu.'
translations.idiomatic: Sui He said, "The King of Han has sent me to respectfully present a letter to Your Majesty. I confess myself puzzled by Your Majesty's close attachment to Chu."

sentences.zh: 淮南王曰：「寡人北鄉而臣事之。」

translations.literal: The King of Huainan said: 'I face north and serve him as a subject.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan replied, "I face north and serve him as a vassal."

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「大王與項王俱列為諸侯，北鄉而臣事之，必以楚為彊，可以託國也。

translations.literal: Sui He said: 'Your Majesty and the King of Xiang were both ranked as feudal lords, yet you face north and serve him as a subject. This must be because you consider Chu strong enough to entrust your kingdom to.
translations.idiomatic: Sui He said, "Your Majesty and the King of Xiang were both enfeoffed as feudal lords, yet you face north and serve him as a vassal. This can only be because you consider Chu powerful enough to entrust with your kingdom's safety.

sentences.zh: 項王伐齊，身負板筑，以為士卒先，大王宜悉淮南之眾，身自將之，為楚軍前鋒，今乃發四千人以助楚。

translations.literal: When the King of Xiang attacked Qi, he personally carried planks and rammed earth, leading the soldiers from the front. Your Majesty ought to have mobilized all the forces of Huainan and personally commanded them as the vanguard of the Chu army. Yet you sent only four thousand men to aid Chu.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Xiang campaigned against Qi, he personally shouldered planks and rammed earth alongside his men, leading from the very front. If you truly serve him, Your Majesty ought to have mustered every soldier in Huainan and personally led them as the vanguard of Chu's army. Instead, you sent a mere four thousand men to assist.

sentences.zh: 夫北面而臣事人者，固若是乎？

translations.literal: Is this really how one who faces north and serves as a subject should behave?
translations.idiomatic: Is this how a man who calls himself a loyal vassal conducts himself?

sentences.zh: 夫漢王戰於彭城，項王未出齊也，大王宜騷淮南之兵渡淮，日夜會戰彭城下，大王撫萬人之眾，無一人渡淮者，垂拱而觀其孰勝。

translations.literal: When the King of Han fought at Pengcheng, the King of Xiang had not yet left Qi. Your Majesty should have mobilized the Huainan army to cross the Huai River and join battle day and night beneath Pengcheng. Yet Your Majesty commanded tens of thousands of troops without sending a single man across the Huai, folding your arms to watch who would win.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Han was fighting at Pengcheng, the King of Xiang was still in Qi. Your Majesty should have rallied the forces of Huainan to cross the Huai River and rush to join battle at Pengcheng day and night. Yet with tens of thousands of troops at your command, you did not send a single soldier across the Huai, content to fold your arms and watch from the sidelines to see who would prevail.

sentences.zh: 夫託國於人者，固若是乎？

translations.literal: Is this really how one who entrusts his kingdom to another should behave?
translations.idiomatic: Is this how a man who entrusts his kingdom to another's protection should act?

sentences.zh: 大王提空名以鄉楚，而欲厚自託，臣竊為大王不取也。

translations.literal: Your Majesty offers only an empty name of allegiance to Chu, while hoping for solid protection in return. I humbly believe Your Majesty's position is untenable.
translations.idiomatic: Your Majesty pledges allegiance to Chu in name only, yet expects the full protection that comes with genuine loyalty. I venture to say that Your Majesty's strategy is deeply flawed.

sentences.zh: 然而大王不背楚者，以漢為弱也。

translations.literal: However, the reason Your Majesty does not turn against Chu is because you consider Han to be weak.
translations.idiomatic: And yet the reason Your Majesty has not turned against Chu is simply that you consider Han to be weak.

sentences.zh: 夫楚兵雖彊，天下負之以不義之名，以其背盟約而殺義帝也。

translations.literal: Although Chu's army is strong, the world bears the name of injustice upon it, because it broke its covenants and killed the Righteous Emperor.
translations.idiomatic: Though the Chu army is powerful, the whole world brands it with the name of injustice, for it violated its sworn covenants and murdered the Righteous Emperor.

sentences.zh: 然而楚王恃戰勝自彊，漢王收諸侯，還守成皋、滎陽，下蜀、漢之粟，深溝壁壘，分卒守徼乘塞，楚人還兵，閒以梁地，深入敵國八九百里，欲戰則不得，攻城則力不能，老弱轉糧千里之外；

translations.literal: Nevertheless, the King of Chu relies on his battle victories to assert his strength. But the King of Han has rallied the feudal lords, returned to defend Chenggao and Xingyang, brought down grain from Shu and Han, dug deep moats and built strong ramparts, deployed troops to guard the passes and barriers. The Chu forces had to withdraw their army, with the territory of Liang between them, having advanced eight or nine hundred li deep into enemy territory. They wished to fight but could not, wished to storm cities but lacked the strength, while the old and weak transported grain from over a thousand li away;
translations.idiomatic: The King of Chu may rely on his battle victories to assert his strength, but the King of Han has rallied the feudal lords, fortified Chenggao and Xingyang, and secured the grain supplies of Shu and Han. He has dug deep trenches, raised high ramparts, and stationed troops at every pass and strategic point. The Chu army, meanwhile, must march through the territory of Liang, advancing eight or nine hundred li deep into hostile territory. They seek battle but cannot force it; they attack fortified cities but lack the strength to take them. Their old and infirm must haul supplies over a thousand li;

sentences.zh: 楚兵至滎陽、成皋，漢堅守而不動，進則不得攻，退則不得解。

translations.literal: When the Chu forces reach Xingyang and Chenggao, Han holds firm and does not budge. If they advance, they cannot attack; if they retreat, they cannot disengage.
translations.idiomatic: When the Chu forces reach Xingyang and Chenggao, Han stands firm and will not yield. If they press forward, they cannot breach the defenses; if they fall back, they cannot safely disengage.

sentences.zh: 故曰楚兵不足恃也。

translations.literal: Therefore I say the Chu army is not something that can be relied upon.
translations.idiomatic: This is why I say the might of Chu is not to be relied upon.

sentences.zh: 使楚勝漢，則諸侯自危懼而相救。

translations.literal: If Chu defeats Han, the other lords will feel endangered and frightened and will come to each other's rescue.
translations.idiomatic: Should Chu defeat Han, the other feudal lords will feel their own safety threatened and will unite to defend one another.

sentences.zh: 夫楚之彊，適足以致天下之兵耳。

translations.literal: Chu's strength is merely sufficient to invite the armies of the entire world against it.
translations.idiomatic: The very strength of Chu will only succeed in drawing the armies of the entire world against it.

sentences.zh: 故楚不如漢，其勢易見也。

translations.literal: Therefore Chu is inferior to Han; this strategic reality is easy to see.
translations.idiomatic: Chu is therefore no match for Han. The strategic reality could not be clearer.

sentences.zh: 今大王不與萬全之漢而自託於危亡之楚，臣竊為大王惑之。

translations.literal: Now Your Majesty does not ally with Han, which is certain of success, but instead entrusts yourself to Chu, which faces ruin. I humbly find this bewildering on Your Majesty's part.
translations.idiomatic: Yet now Your Majesty refuses to side with Han, whose victory is assured, and instead stakes your fate on Chu, which teeters on the brink of destruction. I confess myself bewildered by Your Majesty's reasoning.

sentences.zh: 臣非以淮南之兵足以亡楚也。

translations.literal: I do not say that the forces of Huainan alone are sufficient to destroy Chu.
translations.idiomatic: I do not claim that the forces of Huainan alone could destroy Chu.

sentences.zh: 夫大王發兵而倍楚，項王必留；

translations.literal: If Your Majesty raises troops and betrays Chu, the King of Xiang will certainly be detained;
translations.idiomatic: But if Your Majesty raises an army and turns against Chu, the King of Xiang will be forced to stay and deal with you.

sentences.zh: 留數月，漢之取天下可以萬全。

translations.literal: If he is detained for several months, Han's conquest of the empire can be absolutely certain.
translations.idiomatic: Pin him down for just a few months, and Han's conquest of the empire will be absolutely certain.

sentences.zh: 臣請與大王提劍而歸漢，漢王必裂地而封大王，又況淮南，淮南必大王有也。

translations.literal: I ask to take up the sword alongside Your Majesty and return to Han. The King of Han will certainly carve out territory and enfeoff Your Majesty, and moreover Huainan—Huainan will surely remain Your Majesty's.
translations.idiomatic: Allow me to ride at Your Majesty's side with sword in hand and go over to Han. The King of Han will certainly grant you a fief of carved-out territory, and Huainan itself will assuredly remain yours.

sentences.zh: 故漢王敬使使臣進愚計，願大王之留意也。」

translations.literal: Therefore the King of Han has respectfully sent this subject to present these humble plans, and hopes Your Majesty will give them your attention.'
translations.idiomatic: For these reasons, the King of Han has respectfully dispatched me to present this humble counsel. I beg Your Majesty to consider it carefully."

sentences.zh: 淮南王曰：「請奉命。」

translations.literal: The King of Huainan said: 'I accept the order.'
translations.idiomatic: The King of Huainan said, "I shall comply."

sentences.zh: 陰許畔楚與漢，未敢泄也。

translations.literal: He secretly agreed to betray Chu and side with Han, but did not yet dare to reveal it.
translations.idiomatic: He secretly agreed to defect from Chu and join Han, but dared not yet make his intentions known.

sentences.zh: 楚使者在，方急責英布發兵，舍傳舍。

translations.literal: The Chu envoy was present, urgently pressing Ying Bu to dispatch troops, and was lodged at the guesthouse.
translations.idiomatic: At this time, the Chu envoy was still present, pressing Ying Bu to mobilize his troops, and was staying at the official guesthouse.

sentences.zh: 隨何直入，坐楚使者上坐，曰：「九江王已歸漢，楚何以得發兵？」

translations.literal: Sui He walked straight in and took the seat above the Chu envoy, saying: 'The King of Jiujiang has already submitted to Han. On what grounds can Chu demand troops?'
translations.idiomatic: Sui He strode in unannounced and seated himself in the place of honor above the Chu envoy, declaring, "The King of Jiujiang has already gone over to Han. On what grounds does Chu demand his troops?"

sentences.zh: 布愕然。

translations.literal: Bu was stunned.
translations.idiomatic: Bu was struck dumb with shock.

sentences.zh: 楚使者起。

translations.literal: The Chu envoy rose to leave.
translations.idiomatic: The Chu envoy leapt to his feet.

sentences.zh: 何因說布曰：「事已搆，可遂殺楚使者，無使歸，而疾走漢并力。」

translations.literal: Sui He then urged Bu, saying: 'The matter has already come to a head. You should kill the Chu envoy at once, do not let him return, and quickly go to Han to join forces.'
translations.idiomatic: Sui He then urged Bu, saying, "The deed is already done. You must kill the Chu envoy immediately and prevent him from returning. Then make haste to join forces with Han."

sentences.zh: 布曰：「如使者教，因起兵而擊之耳。」

translations.literal: Bu said: 'I will follow the envoy's advice, and raise troops to attack them.'
translations.idiomatic: Bu said, "I shall follow your counsel and raise my army to strike against Chu."

sentences.zh: 於是殺使者，因起兵而攻楚。

translations.literal: Thereupon he killed the envoy, and raised troops to attack Chu.
translations.idiomatic: With that, he had the Chu envoy killed, raised his army, and launched an attack on Chu.

sentences.zh: 楚使項聲、龍且攻淮南，項王留而攻下邑。

translations.literal: Chu sent Xiang Sheng and Long Ju to attack Huainan, while the King of Xiang stayed behind to attack Xiayi.
translations.idiomatic: Chu dispatched Xiang Sheng and Long Ju to attack Huainan, while the King of Xiang remained behind to assault Xiayi.

sentences.zh: 數月，龍且擊淮南，破布軍。

translations.literal: After several months, Long Ju attacked Huainan and defeated Bu's army.
translations.idiomatic: After several months of fighting, Long Ju attacked Huainan and routed Bu's forces.

sentences.zh: 布欲引兵走漢，恐楚王殺之，故閒行與何俱歸漢。

translations.literal: Bu wanted to lead his troops and flee to Han, but feared the King of Chu would kill him, so he traveled secretly with Sui He and returned to Han together.
translations.idiomatic: Bu wished to lead his forces to join Han, but feared the King of Chu would have him killed. He therefore slipped away in secret and traveled with Sui He back to the Han court.

sentences.zh: 淮南王至，上方踞床洗，召布入見，布（甚）大怒，悔來，欲自殺。

translations.literal: When the King of Huainan arrived, the Emperor was sitting on his bed having his feet washed. He summoned Bu for an audience. Bu flew into a great rage, regretted coming, and wished to kill himself.
translations.idiomatic: When the King of Huainan arrived, the Emperor was lounging on his couch having his feet washed. He summoned Bu in for an audience in this state. Bu was seized with fury and regretted having come, nearly wanting to take his own life.

sentences.zh: 出就舍，帳御飲食從官如漢王居，布又大喜過望。

translations.literal: But when he went out to his lodgings, the curtains, attendants, food, and retinue were the same as those of the King of Han's own quarters. Bu was again overjoyed beyond expectation.
translations.idiomatic: But when he retired to his quarters, he found the furnishings, attendants, food, and retinue identical to those of the King of Han himself. Bu was overjoyed beyond all expectation.

sentences.zh: 於是乃使人入九江。

translations.literal: Thereupon he sent men into Jiujiang.
translations.idiomatic: He then sent agents back into Jiujiang to rally his remaining forces.

sentences.zh: 楚已使項伯收九江兵，盡殺布妻子。

translations.literal: Chu had already sent Xiang Bo to seize the Jiujiang army and had killed all of Bu's wife and children.
translations.idiomatic: But Chu had already dispatched Xiang Bo to seize the Jiujiang army and had put Bu's wife and children to death.

sentences.zh: 布使者頗得故人幸臣，將眾數千人歸漢。

translations.literal: Bu's envoys managed to gather some of his old friends and favored ministers, and led several thousand men back to Han.
translations.idiomatic: Bu's agents managed to rally a number of his old friends and trusted ministers, and led several thousand men back to the Han side.

sentences.zh: 漢益分布兵而與俱北，收兵至成皋。

translations.literal: Han additionally allocated troops to Bu and together they went north, gathering soldiers until they reached Chenggao.
translations.idiomatic: Han reinforced Bu with additional troops, and together they marched northward, recruiting soldiers along the way until they reached Chenggao.

sentences.zh: 四年七月，立布為淮南王，與擊項籍。

translations.literal: In the seventh month of the fourth year, Bu was established as the King of Huainan and joined in the attack on Xiang Ji.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh month of the fourth year, Bu was formally installed as the King of Huainan and joined the campaign against Xiang Ji.

sentences.zh: 漢五年，布使人入九江，得數縣。

translations.literal: In the fifth year of Han, Bu sent men into Jiujiang and recovered several counties.
translations.idiomatic: In the fifth year of Han, Bu sent agents into Jiujiang and recovered several counties.

sentences.zh: 六年，布與劉賈入九江，誘大司馬周殷，周殷反楚，遂舉九江兵與漢擊楚，破之垓下。

translations.literal: In the sixth year, Bu and Liu Jia entered Jiujiang and lured the Grand Marshal Zhou Yin over. Zhou Yin turned against Chu, and they thereupon raised the Jiujiang army to join Han in attacking Chu, defeating them at Gaixia.
translations.idiomatic: In the sixth year, Bu and Liu Jia entered Jiujiang and won over the Grand Marshal Zhou Yin to their cause. Zhou Yin defected from Chu, and together they raised the entire Jiujiang army to join Han in the final assault on Chu, crushing them at Gaixia.

sentences.zh: 項籍死，天下定，上置酒。

translations.literal: Xiang Ji died, the empire was pacified, and the Emperor held a banquet.
translations.idiomatic: After Xiang Ji's death, the empire was at last pacified, and the Emperor held a grand banquet.

sentences.zh: 上折隨何之功，謂何為腐儒，為天下安用腐儒。

translations.literal: The Emperor belittled Sui He's achievements, called him a rotten scholar, and asked what use the world had for rotten scholars.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor dismissed Sui He's achievements, called him a pedantic scholar, and asked what use the empire had for pedantic scholars.

sentences.zh: 隨何跪曰：「夫陛下引兵攻彭城，楚王未去齊也，陛下發步卒五萬人，騎五千，能以取淮南乎？」

translations.literal: Sui He knelt and said: 'When Your Majesty led the army to attack Pengcheng while the King of Chu had not yet left Qi, if Your Majesty had dispatched fifty thousand infantry and five thousand cavalry, could you have taken Huainan?'
translations.idiomatic: Sui He knelt and said, "When Your Majesty led the army against Pengcheng while the King of Chu was still in Qi, could you have taken Huainan with fifty thousand foot soldiers and five thousand cavalry?"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「不能。」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'I could not.'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor admitted, "I could not."

sentences.zh: 隨何曰：「陛下使何與二十人使淮南，至，如陛下之意，是何之功賢於步卒五萬人騎五千也。

translations.literal: Sui He said: 'Your Majesty sent me with twenty men as envoys to Huainan. Upon our arrival, everything went as Your Majesty wished. Is not my achievement then worth more than fifty thousand infantry and five thousand cavalry?
translations.idiomatic: Sui He said, "Your Majesty sent me with a mere twenty men on a mission to Huainan, and everything went exactly as you desired. Is my achievement not worth more than fifty thousand infantry and five thousand cavalry?

sentences.zh: 然而陛下謂何腐儒，為天下安用腐儒，何也？」

translations.literal: Yet Your Majesty calls me a rotten scholar and asks what use the world has for rotten scholars—why is this?'
translations.idiomatic: Yet Your Majesty calls me a pedantic scholar and asks what use the empire has for such men. Why?"

sentences.zh: 上曰：「吾方圖子之功。」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'I was just about to consider your achievements.'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor said, "I was just about to consider your merits for a reward."

sentences.zh: 乃以隨何為護軍中尉。

translations.literal: He then appointed Sui He as the Protector of the Army and Colonel of the Capital.
translations.idiomatic: He thereupon appointed Sui He as Colonel and Protector of the Army.

sentences.zh: 布遂剖符為淮南王，都六，九江、廬江、衡山、豫章郡皆屬布。

translations.literal: Bu then received a split tally as the King of Huainan, with his capital at Liu. The commanderies of Jiujiang, Lujiang, Hengshan, and Yuzhang all belonged to Bu.
translations.idiomatic: Bu was thus formally invested with the split tally as the King of Huainan, with his capital at Liu. The commanderies of Jiujiang, Lujiang, Hengshan, and Yuzhang were all placed under his rule.

sentences.zh: 七年，朝陳。

translations.literal: In the seventh year, he attended court at Chen.
translations.idiomatic: In the seventh year, he went to pay court at Chen.

sentences.zh: 八年，朝雒陽。

translations.literal: In the eighth year, he attended court at Luoyang.
translations.idiomatic: In the eighth year, he paid court at Luoyang.

sentences.zh: 九年，朝長安。

translations.literal: In the ninth year, he attended court at Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: In the ninth year, he paid court at Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 十一年，高后誅淮陰侯，布因心恐。

translations.literal: In the eleventh year, Empress Dowager Gao executed the Marquis of Huaiyin, and Bu became fearful at heart.
translations.idiomatic: In the eleventh year, Empress Gao had the Marquis of Huaiyin executed, and Bu grew fearful in his heart.

sentences.zh: 夏，漢誅梁王彭越，醢之，盛其醢遍賜諸侯。

translations.literal: In the summer, Han executed Peng Yue, the King of Liang, minced his flesh into paste, placed the paste in vessels, and distributed it to all the feudal lords.
translations.idiomatic: That summer, Han executed Peng Yue, the King of Liang, had his flesh minced into a paste, and sent portions of it to every feudal lord in the realm.

sentences.zh: 至淮南，淮南王方獵，見醢，因大恐，陰令人部聚兵，候伺旁郡警急。

translations.literal: When the paste arrived in Huainan, the King of Huainan was hunting. Upon seeing the paste, he was seized with great terror. He secretly ordered men to assemble troops and watch for any alarms or emergencies in the neighboring commanderies.
translations.idiomatic: When the parcel arrived in Huainan, the King of Huainan was out hunting. Upon seeing the flesh paste, he was seized with terror. He secretly ordered his men to assemble troops and keep watch for signs of military action in the neighboring commanderies.

sentences.zh: 布所幸姬疾，請就醫，醫家與中大夫賁赫對門，姬數如醫家，賁赫自以為侍中，乃厚餽遺，從姬飲醫家。

translations.literal: A concubine favored by Bu fell ill and requested to see a physician. The physician's house was directly across from that of the Palace Grandee Ben He. The concubine visited the physician's house frequently. Ben He, considering himself a palace attendant, sent her generous gifts and followed the concubine to drink at the physician's house.
translations.idiomatic: One of Bu's favored concubines fell ill and asked permission to visit a physician. The physician's house happened to be directly opposite the residence of Ben He, a Palace Grandee. As the concubine visited the physician frequently, Ben He—considering his own rank as a court attendant—sent her lavish gifts and joined her for drinks at the physician's house.

sentences.zh: 姬侍王，從容語次，譽赫長者也。

translations.literal: While attending the king, the concubine casually praised He as a worthy elder during conversation.
translations.idiomatic: While attending the king, the concubine casually praised He in conversation as a man of fine character.

sentences.zh: 王怒曰：「汝安從知之？」

translations.literal: The king said angrily: 'How would you know such things about him?'
translations.idiomatic: The king said angrily, "How would you come to know anything about him?"

sentences.zh: 具說狀。

translations.literal: She described the situation in full.
translations.idiomatic: The concubine explained the entire situation in detail.

sentences.zh: 王疑其與亂。

translations.literal: The king suspected that He was having an affair with her.
translations.idiomatic: The king suspected that He had been carrying on an affair with her.

sentences.zh: 赫恐，稱病。

translations.literal: He grew frightened and pleaded illness.
translations.idiomatic: Ben He grew frightened and pleaded illness to avoid the king.

sentences.zh: 王愈怒，欲捕赫。

translations.literal: The king grew even more furious and wished to arrest He.
translations.idiomatic: The king's fury only deepened, and he resolved to have He arrested.

sentences.zh: 赫言變事，乘傳詣長安。

translations.literal: He reported a treasonous plot, took a relay carriage, and went to Chang'an.
translations.idiomatic: He denounced a treasonous plot and commandeered a relay carriage to flee to Chang'an.

sentences.zh: 布使人追，不及。

translations.literal: Bu sent men to pursue him, but they could not catch him.
translations.idiomatic: Bu sent men in pursuit, but they failed to catch him.

sentences.zh: 赫至，上變，言布謀反有端，可先未發誅也。

translations.literal: When He arrived, he reported the treason, saying that Bu's plot to rebel had clear signs and that he could be punished before he acted.
translations.idiomatic: When He reached the capital, he reported the plot, declaring that Bu's rebellion was taking shape and that he should be struck down before he could act.

sentences.zh: 上讀其書，語蕭相國。

translations.literal: The Emperor read his memorial and discussed it with Chancellor Xiao.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor read the memorial and consulted Chancellor Xiao.

sentences.zh: 相國曰：「布不宜有此，恐仇怨妄誣之。

translations.literal: The Chancellor said: 'It is unlikely that Bu would do such a thing. I fear that a personal enemy has falsely accused him.
translations.idiomatic: The Chancellor said, "It is unlikely Bu would do such a thing. I fear a personal enemy has falsely accused him.

sentences.zh: 請擊赫，使人微驗淮南王。」

translations.literal: I recommend arresting He, and sending someone to secretly investigate the King of Huainan.'
translations.idiomatic: I recommend having He arrested, and dispatching someone to quietly investigate the King of Huainan."

sentences.zh: 淮南王布見赫以罪亡，上變，固已疑其言國陰事；

translations.literal: The King of Huainan, Bu, saw that He had fled on account of a crime and reported treason, and already suspected that He had revealed the kingdom's secret affairs;
translations.idiomatic: When Bu, the King of Huainan, learned that He had fled under threat of arrest and reported treason to the court, he already suspected that He had revealed the kingdom's secret plans.

sentences.zh: 漢使又來，頗有所驗，遂族赫家，發兵反。

translations.literal: When Han's envoys came again and seemed to be investigating, he thereupon exterminated He's entire clan and raised troops in rebellion.
translations.idiomatic: When the Han envoys arrived and seemed to be probing into the matter, Bu had He's entire clan executed and raised his troops in open rebellion.

sentences.zh: 反書聞，上乃赦賁赫，以為將軍。

translations.literal: When news of the rebellion reached the court, the Emperor pardoned Ben He and appointed him as a general.
translations.idiomatic: When the report of Bu's rebellion reached the capital, the Emperor pardoned Ben He and appointed him as a general.

sentences.zh: 上召諸將問曰：「布反，為之柰何？」

translations.literal: The Emperor summoned his generals and asked: 'Bu has rebelled. What shall we do about it?'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor summoned his generals and asked, "Bu has rebelled. What is to be done?"

sentences.zh: 皆曰；

translations.literal: They all said,
translations.idiomatic: They all replied,

sentences.zh: 「發兵擊之，阬豎子耳。

translations.literal: 'Send troops to attack him and bury the wretch alive.
translations.idiomatic: "Send troops to crush him and bury the wretch alive.

sentences.zh: 何能為乎！」

translations.literal: What can he possibly do!'
translations.idiomatic: What can he possibly do!"

sentences.zh: 汝陰侯滕公召故楚令尹問之。

translations.literal: The Marquis of Ruyin, Lord Teng, summoned the former Chu Chief Minister and asked his opinion.
translations.idiomatic: The Marquis of Ruyin, Lord Teng, summoned the former Chief Minister of Chu and consulted him on the matter.

sentences.zh: 令尹曰：「是故當反。」

translations.literal: The Chief Minister said: 'This is precisely why he had to rebel.'
translations.idiomatic: The Chief Minister said, "It was inevitable that he would rebel."

sentences.zh: 滕公曰：「上裂地而王之，疏爵而貴之，南面而立萬乘之主，其反何也？」

translations.literal: Lord Teng said: 'The Emperor carved out territory and made him a king, bestowed a noble title to honor him, and set him up as ruler of ten thousand chariots facing south. Why then did he rebel?'
translations.idiomatic: Lord Teng asked, "The Emperor carved out territory and made him a king, bestowed a noble title upon him, and set him up as a ruler of ten thousand chariots. Why would he rebel?"

sentences.zh: 令尹曰：「往年殺彭越，前年殺韓信，此三人者，同功一體之人也。

translations.literal: The Chief Minister said: 'Last year Peng Yue was killed; the year before, Han Xin was killed. These three men shared the same achievements and were of one body.
translations.idiomatic: The Chief Minister replied, "Last year Peng Yue was killed; the year before that, Han Xin was killed. These three men shared the same achievements and the same fate.

sentences.zh: 自疑禍及身，故反耳。」

translations.literal: He suspected that disaster would reach him as well, so he rebelled.'
translations.idiomatic: He feared that catastrophe would come for him next, and so he rebelled."

sentences.zh: 滕公言之上曰：「臣客故楚令尹薛公者，其人有籌筴之計，可問。」

translations.literal: Lord Teng reported to the Emperor: 'My retainer, the former Chu Chief Minister Duke of Xue, is a man of great strategic ability. He may be consulted.'
translations.idiomatic: Lord Teng reported to the Emperor, "My retainer, the former Chu Chief Minister known as the Duke of Xue, is a man of great strategic insight. He should be consulted."

sentences.zh: 上乃召見問薛公。

translations.literal: The Emperor then summoned and questioned the Duke of Xue.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor summoned the Duke of Xue for an audience and questioned him.

sentences.zh: 薛公對曰：「布反不足怪也。

translations.literal: The Duke of Xue replied: 'Bu's rebellion is nothing to be surprised at.
translations.idiomatic: The Duke of Xue replied, "Bu's rebellion should come as no surprise.

sentences.zh: 使布出於上計，山東非漢之有也；

translations.literal: If Bu follows the best strategy, the lands east of the mountains will no longer belong to Han;
translations.idiomatic: If Bu adopts the best strategy, the lands east of the mountains will be lost to Han.

sentences.zh: 出於中計，勝敗之數未可知也；

translations.literal: If he follows the middle strategy, the outcome of victory or defeat cannot be predicted;
translations.idiomatic: If he follows the middle strategy, the outcome will be uncertain.

sentences.zh: 出於下計，陛下安枕而臥矣。」

translations.literal: If he follows the worst strategy, Your Majesty may rest your head on the pillow and sleep peacefully.'
translations.idiomatic: If he follows the worst strategy, Your Majesty may sleep soundly."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「何謂上計？」

translations.literal: The Emperor asked: 'What is the best strategy?'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor asked, "What would the best strategy be?"

sentences.zh: 令尹對曰：「東取吳，西取楚，并齊取魯，傳檄燕、趙，固守其所，山東非漢之有也。」

translations.literal: The Chief Minister replied: 'Take Wu in the east, take Chu in the west, annex Qi and seize Lu, send proclamations to Yan and Zhao, and hold firm where he stands. The lands east of the mountains would no longer belong to Han.'
translations.idiomatic: The Chief Minister replied, "Seize Wu to the east, take Chu to the west, annex Qi and conquer Lu, send proclamations rallying Yan and Zhao, then hold his ground. The lands east of the mountains would be lost to Han."

sentences.zh: 「何謂中計？」

translations.literal: 'What is the middle strategy?'
translations.idiomatic: "What would the middle strategy be?"

sentences.zh: 「東取吳，西取楚，并韓取魏，據敖庾之粟，塞成皋之口，勝敗之數未可知也。」

translations.literal: 'Take Wu in the east, take Chu in the west, annex Han and seize Wei, seize the grain stores at Ao Granary, and block the pass at Chenggao. Then the outcome of victory or defeat cannot be known.'
translations.idiomatic: "Seize Wu to the east, take Chu to the west, annex Han and conquer Wei, secure the grain stores at Ao Granary, and block the pass at Chenggao. Then the outcome would hang in the balance."

sentences.zh: 「何謂下計？」

translations.literal: 'What is the worst strategy?'
translations.idiomatic: "And the worst strategy?"

sentences.zh: 「東取吳，西取下蔡，歸重於越，身歸長沙，陛下安枕而臥，漢無事矣。」

translations.literal: 'Take Wu in the east, take Xiacai in the west, send his valuables south to Yue, and retreat personally to Changsha. Then Your Majesty may rest your head on the pillow and sleep, and Han will have nothing to worry about.'
translations.idiomatic: "Seize Wu to the east, take Xiacai to the west, send his treasures to Yue for safekeeping, and retreat to Changsha himself. In that case, Your Majesty may sleep soundly, and the Han empire will be secure."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「是計將安出？」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'Which strategy will he adopt?'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor asked, "Which strategy will he choose?"

sentences.zh: 令尹對曰：「出下計。」

translations.literal: The Chief Minister replied: 'He will adopt the worst strategy.'
translations.idiomatic: The Chief Minister replied, "He will choose the worst one."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「何謂廢上中計而出下計？」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'Why would he abandon the best and middle strategies and adopt the worst?'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor asked, "Why would he pass over the best and middle strategies and choose the worst?"

sentences.zh: 令尹曰：「布故麗山之徒也，自致萬乘之主，此皆為身，不顧後為百姓萬世慮者也，故曰出下計。」

translations.literal: The Chief Minister said: 'Bu was originally a convict laborer at Mount Li who raised himself to become the lord of ten thousand chariots. All of this was done for himself alone; he has never thought of the people or planned for ten thousand generations. Therefore I say he will adopt the worst strategy.'
translations.idiomatic: The Chief Minister said, "Bu was once a convict laborer at Mount Li who raised himself to become the ruler of ten thousand chariots. Everything he has done has been for himself alone—he has never given a thought to the welfare of the people or planned for future generations. This is why I say he will choose the worst strategy."

sentences.zh: 上曰：「善。」

translations.literal: The Emperor said: 'Good.'
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor said, "Well spoken."

sentences.zh: 封薛公千戶。

translations.literal: He enfeoffed the Duke of Xue with a fief of one thousand households.
translations.idiomatic: He rewarded the Duke of Xue with a fief of one thousand households.

sentences.zh: 乃立皇子長為淮南王。

translations.literal: He then established the imperial prince Chang as King of Huainan.
translations.idiomatic: He then installed the imperial prince Chang as the new King of Huainan.

sentences.zh: 上遂發兵自將東擊布。

translations.literal: The Emperor thereupon raised troops and personally led them eastward to attack Bu.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor then raised an army and personally led the campaign eastward against Bu.

sentences.zh: 布之初反，謂其將曰：「上老矣，厭兵，必不能來。

translations.literal: When Bu first rebelled, he told his generals: 'The Emperor is old and weary of war. He certainly will not come in person.
translations.idiomatic: When Bu first raised his rebellion, he told his generals, "The Emperor is old and weary of war. He will certainly not come in person.

sentences.zh: 使諸將，諸將獨患淮陰、彭越，今皆已死，餘不足畏也。」

translations.literal: If he sends his generals, the only generals to worry about were the Marquis of Huaiyin and Peng Yue—both are now dead. The rest are not worth fearing.'
translations.idiomatic: If he sends his generals, the only ones to fear were the Marquis of Huaiyin and Peng Yue—both are now dead. The rest are not worth worrying about."

sentences.zh: 故遂反。

translations.literal: Therefore he proceeded to rebel.
translations.idiomatic: And so he went ahead with the rebellion.

sentences.zh: 果如薛公籌之，東擊荊，荊王劉賈走死富陵。

translations.literal: Indeed, just as the Duke of Xue had predicted, Bu struck eastward against Jing. The King of Jing, Liu Jia, fled and died at Fuling.
translations.idiomatic: Just as the Duke of Xue had predicted, Bu struck eastward against Jing. The King of Jing, Liu Jia, fled and met his death at Fuling.

sentences.zh: 盡劫其兵，渡淮擊楚。

translations.literal: He seized all of Jing's troops, crossed the Huai River, and attacked Chu.
translations.idiomatic: He commandeered all of Jing's soldiers, crossed the Huai River, and launched an attack on the kingdom of Chu.

sentences.zh: 楚發兵與戰徐、僮閒，為三軍，欲以相救為奇。

translations.literal: Chu raised troops and fought between Xu and Tong, forming three armies with the intention of using mutual reinforcement as a surprise tactic.
translations.idiomatic: The kingdom of Chu raised an army and engaged him between Xu and Tong, dividing their forces into three columns with a plan of mutual reinforcement as a surprise stratagem.

sentences.zh: 或說楚將曰：「布善用兵，民素畏之。

translations.literal: Someone advised the Chu general, saying: 'Bu is skilled at using troops, and the people have long feared him.
translations.idiomatic: Someone warned the Chu general, saying, "Bu is a master of warfare, and the people have long feared him.

sentences.zh: 且兵法，諸侯戰其地為散地。

translations.literal: Moreover, according to the art of war, when lords fight on their own territory, it is called 'dispersed ground.'
translations.idiomatic: Moreover, the art of war teaches that when lords fight on their own territory, it is called dispersed ground.

sentences.zh: 今別為三，彼敗吾一軍，餘皆走，安能相救！」

translations.literal: Now if we divide into three and he defeats one of our armies, the rest will all flee. How could they come to each other's rescue!'
translations.idiomatic: If we split into three and he routs just one of our columns, the others will scatter and run. How could they possibly come to one another's rescue?"

sentences.zh: 不聽。

translations.literal: They did not listen.
translations.idiomatic: The general did not heed this advice.

sentences.zh: 布果破其一軍，其二軍散走。

translations.literal: Bu indeed defeated one of their armies, and the other two scattered and fled.
translations.idiomatic: Sure enough, Bu routed one of the columns, and the other two scattered and fled.

sentences.zh: 遂西，與上兵遇蘄西，會甀。

translations.literal: He then advanced west and encountered the Emperor's army west of Qi, meeting at Zhui.
translations.idiomatic: He then pushed westward and encountered the Emperor's army west of Qi, joining battle at Zhui.

sentences.zh: 布兵精甚，上乃壁庸城，望布軍置陳如項籍軍，上惡之。

translations.literal: Bu's troops were extremely elite. The Emperor fortified his position at Yong City and observed Bu's army deploying in formations like those of Xiang Ji's army. The Emperor was dismayed.
translations.idiomatic: Bu's forces were formidable in their discipline. The Emperor fortified his position at Yong City, and when he observed Bu's formations arranged in the same manner as Xiang Ji had once deployed his troops, he was deeply troubled.

sentences.zh: 與布相望見，遙謂布曰：「何苦而反？」

translations.literal: Facing Bu across the battlefield, he called out from a distance: 'Why have you gone to such lengths to rebel?'
translations.idiomatic: Facing Bu across the lines, the Emperor called out from a distance, "What drove you to rebel?"

sentences.zh: 布曰：「欲為帝耳。」

translations.literal: Bu said: 'I wish to become emperor, nothing more.'
translations.idiomatic: Bu replied, "I wanted to be emperor, nothing more."

sentences.zh: 上怒罵之，遂大戰。

translations.literal: The Emperor cursed him in fury, and thereupon a great battle ensued.
translations.idiomatic: The Emperor cursed him in fury, and a great battle erupted.

sentences.zh: 布軍敗走，渡淮，數止戰，不利，與百餘人走江南。

translations.literal: Bu's army was defeated and fled. He crossed the Huai River, stopped several times to fight, but each time was unsuccessful. He fled south of the Yangtze with only a hundred or so men.
translations.idiomatic: Bu's army was routed and fled. He crossed the Huai River, making several stands along the way, but each time without success. In the end he fled south of the Yangtze with barely a hundred men.

sentences.zh: 布故與番君婚，以故長沙哀王使人紿布，偽與亡，誘走越，故信而隨之番陽。

translations.literal: Bu had formerly been connected by marriage to Lord Fan. For this reason, King Ai of Changsha sent someone to deceive Bu, pretending to flee with him and luring him toward Yue. Bu trusted this and followed the person to Fanyang.
translations.idiomatic: Bu had once been connected by marriage to Lord Fan of Poyang. Taking advantage of this, King Ai of Changsha sent an agent to deceive Bu, pretending to flee with him and luring him toward the kingdom of Yue. Bu trusted the ruse and followed the agent to Fanyang.

sentences.zh: 番陽人殺布茲鄉民田舍，遂滅黥布。

translations.literal: The people of Fanyang killed Bu at the farmstead of a commoner in Zi Township, and thus Qing Bu was destroyed.
translations.idiomatic: The people of Fanyang killed Bu at the farmstead of a villager in Zi Township. And so ended the life of Qing Bu.

sentences.zh: 立皇子長為淮南王，封賁赫為期思侯，諸將率多以功封者。

translations.literal: The imperial prince Chang was established as King of Huainan. Ben He was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Qisi. Most of the generals were likewise enfeoffed for their merits.
translations.idiomatic: The imperial prince Chang was installed as the King of Huainan, and Ben He was enfeoffed as the Marquis of Qisi. Most of the generals who had served in the campaign likewise received fiefs for their contributions.

sentences.zh: 太史公曰：英布者，其先豈春秋所見楚滅英、六，皋陶之後哉？

translations.literal: The Grand Historian remarks: As for Ying Bu, could his ancestors have been those recorded in the Spring and Autumn Annals when Chu destroyed the states of Ying and Liu—descendants of Gaoyao?
translations.idiomatic: The Grand Historian remarks: Could Ying Bu's ancestors have been those recorded in the Spring and Autumn Annals, from the states of Ying and Liu that Chu destroyed—descendants of the ancient sage Gaoyao?

sentences.zh: 身被刑法，何其拔興之暴也！

translations.literal: He himself suffered the punishment of branding. How sudden and violent was his rise to prominence!
translations.idiomatic: He bore the brand of a convict upon his face. How sudden and violent was his rise!

sentences.zh: 項氏之所阬殺人以千萬數，而布常為首虐。

translations.literal: The Xiang clan's massacres numbered victims in the tens of millions, and Bu was always the chief perpetrator of these atrocities.
translations.idiomatic: When the Xiang clan slaughtered men by the tens of thousands, Bu was always at the forefront of the carnage.

sentences.zh: 功冠諸侯，用此得王，亦不免於身為世大僇。

translations.literal: His achievements surpassed those of all the feudal lords, and by these he gained a kingdom, yet he could not escape becoming a great disgrace to the world in his own person.
translations.idiomatic: His achievements surpassed those of every feudal lord, and through them he won a kingdom. Yet in the end, he could not escape becoming a figure of great shame before the world.

sentences.zh: 禍之興自愛姬殖，妒媢生患，竟以滅國！

translations.literal: The disaster arose from his beloved concubine's actions, jealousy and envy bred calamity, and in the end it destroyed his kingdom!
translations.idiomatic: The seeds of his downfall were sown by a beloved concubine; jealousy and suspicion bred calamity, and in the end they brought about the destruction of his kingdom!
